text:
Negotiation: Part 4 (④, Kōshō (Yon)) is the 410 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Morena sternly asks Borksen to confirm her choice one final time, telling her she cannot change her answer after this. Borksen looks Morena in the eyes, and expressionlessly confirms that it was her final choice. Internally, Borksen shouts and tries to fight back, not wanting to confirm "Yes" as her final choice. Borksen tells herself to stop, that it wasn't true that she wants to choose the "Yes" card, but points to it and confirms that the "Yes" card is the card she chose for her final card. Morena accepts Borksen's response and tells her that she is happy that she would join them, but that she was also a little disappointed.

Using another negative question, Morena guesses that Borksen cheated. Borksen continues looking forward, expressionless. The Heil-Ly underlings who were previously observing the game return and surround Borksen, as Morena suggests that Borksen cheated after using the "Deal" card, by marking the "Return" card with a tiny indentation on the front along both sides when she stopped the shuffle and double-checked the two cards on her side, a mark too far for Morena to see, and one that Orarge would not see when shuffling, since he only sees the backs of the cards. As Morena suggests that Borksen crushed the card in her moment of victory to get rid of the evidence, Borksen continues maintaining her expressionless face.

Morena reveals that the card game and the cards themselves are a part of her ability, which has a manipulative aspect that automatically triggers if Morena's opponent cheats, forcing them to only pick Yes or No. As she does so, she walks around the table to join Borksen, and leans on the table before telling Borksen that even though her choices for the last card were reduced to two, she only had herself to blame for ultimately choosing to cheat. She leans in close to Borksen, who still remains expressionless, and tells her that she should have intuited that cheating is tantamount to abandoning their serious game.

Morena moves to the side and asks Borksen once more, that if by "Yes", she would join the Heil-Ly. Borksen affirms that she will join them, and the Heil-Ly underlings applaud. Borksen finally finds herself released, and falls to her knees. She looks up to see Morena in front of her, who begins to explain the "Yes?" card that Borksen did not choose during the game, to welcome the brand-new member of their team. Morena explains that as long as Borksen is alive and on the Heil-Ly's side, the Heil-Ly would not come after her or the people dear to her. She explains her ability in more detail, revealing its name to her, and describing it like a game app installed in her, with play data that Morena manages. Morena tells Borksen that she always knows Borksen's level, number of points, location, and status, and tells Borksen that the game cannot be removed, continuing until she either clears the game, the admin ceases to exist, or the "phone" is destroyed.

Morena reminds Borksen that she had already explained the three conditions that need to be cleared before the process of joining the Heil-Ly is complete, and that after that, she was largely free to do as she wishes. Morena's gaze intensifies, and she tells Borksen that infighting and backstabbing are not forbidden as long as it was not too flagrant. Borksen gets up onto one knee as Morena tells her that she is currently at Level 0, not yet having fulfilled all the conditions of joining, but would be formally at Level 1 once the final condition of witnessing a murder by a Heil-Ly member is cleared. Morena continues explaining the level system to Borksen, ending her explanation by telling Borksen that while things like leveling up and her choice to remain with them or return to her friends was up to her, the Heil-Ly would betray her the moment they realize that she is of no use to them, and reminding her that their goal is to kill people.

Borksen keeps in mind that she has an undetermined time limit, that she would no longer be valuable to the Heil-Ly if they find another specialist candidate. She decides to assume that Morena installing her ability in her means that it comes with spyware that lets Morena see everything she sees and hears. She remains aware that she needs power to go against them, but also remains aware that the quickest way to get an ability involves killing someone. Borksen gets up, faces Morena, and declares that she is going back to her people. Morena reminds her to remember that the Heil-Ly are now their people as well, and Borksen turns and leaves with a newfound purpose. As she walks away, Borksen looks back, and asks Morena to show her around, saying that she wants to know more about her new comrades before going back.

On Tier 3, orders are repeated for civilians to not move until they have been patted down. Civilians are reminded that they will be shot without warning if they move even an inch, and that they were in the middle of special martial law, not a drill. Soldiers stand along the hallways and staircases, watching over the civilians as they kneel facing the wall with their hands against the walls. Other soldiers watch civilians as they walk through the central hallway with hands behind their heads, slowly escorted away by more soldiers. A corporal communicates over his headset that the Tier 3 central area is clear before a soldier comes up to him, telling his captain that they had found an "Case S".

The corporal is led to Room 3101, which has been covered with cordon tape and has a barricade in front of its door, with two armed soldiers standing guard outside. The soldiers standing guard salute him as he approaches, as the corporal is briefed about the room. The corporal learns that three soldiers had entered the room and disappeared one after another, which the soldier who witnessed it saying that "as soon as they entered the room, they instantly vanished", a textbook supernatural incident. The corporal suggests that it might explain the missing corporal Although there is no confirmation, it is possible that this refers to Corporal Maizan. and soldiers, as one of the soldiers standing guard at the door lets him know that a hidden space behind the bathroom in the room next door had been found, looking like someone cut through the bathroom door. The corporal hears that the hidden room was connected to Room 125, A tweet from Togashi's X account on 21/12/2024 mentions that Room 125 is incorrect, the room in reference during the conversation is meant to be Room 3101. This is intended to be corrected during the volume release of Chapter 410. and the bathroom wall of Room 125 was the trick door. Because Tier 3 is the Heil-Ly's territory, he gives instructions for Tier 1 to be contacted.

1 st Prince Benjamin receives word that what looks to be the Heil-Ly's hideout has been found, and that Nen was involved somehow. He hears that the room was empty and that the soldiers are searching for other hideout locations. He gives orders for Squad 3 to deal with the Heil-Ly while the rest of them prioritize securing the other Princes, and orders his soldiers to find the currently-unaccounted-for 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei and 7 th Prince Luzurus before they focus on the lower-ranking Princes. A soldier reports that Zhang Lei went to Tier 2 right before the special martial law declaration through a cross-bridge from inside Xi-Yu boss Onior's residence, and that they were currently confirming the route and following his trail, with a follow-up report expected in 15 minutes. Benjamin informs his soldiers that Zhang Lei likely sought refuge at the Justice Department and informs them of the possibility that he went farther down to meet up with the mafia. He gives instructions for the Squad 2 reconnaissance team and scouts to focus on Zhang Lei.

As Benjamin makes his way to the Justice Bureau, he keeps in mind that once symptoms begin to appear, he will fall into a coma in 10 hours, and then die 2 hours after that. He estimates that he has about 9.5 more hours, and prepares to eliminate the remaining Princes before that time is up. He hopes that he can inherit the rights of succession officially, which would allow him to pass on the throne to his child even if he dies, in line with Article 1 of the State of Emergency Act: "Crisis of Preserving the Monarchy". A soldier informs the Justice Bureau that Benjamin has arrived, and they escort him in, where Mizaistom, Botobai, and Kaiser wait with their hands up to receive him.

Benjamin introduces himself and cuts to the chase. He tells them that he plans to immediately consolidate all three branches of government by military force at the central court, and that he plans to take over the Justice Bureau building as their joint force command center, before telling them that the three of them would be his witnesses.

Mizaistom asks Benjamin to provide his basis for declaring special martial law, and the letter of authorization from the King. Benjamin informs them that his basis is terrorism with a biological weapon, orchestrated by 4 th Prince Tserriednich and 9 th Prince Halkenburg, using a sample under development brought on board by a collaborator in his military. Currently most likely to be a reference to Balsamilco. Benjamin tells them that there were multiple co-conspirators, and that several suspects had been arrested and are being questioned. He says that some of the suspects were accompanying the Princes' security teams when the Princes were taken to secure locations, and asks for the administrative steps to be completed without delay to be safe.

As Mizaistom listens, he realizes that Benjamin's plan is to leverage the Princes in Tier 1 as hostages to secure the three branches, and sees that Benjamin is, true to his profile, an impulsive dictator who prefers shortcuts, guessing that most of what Benjamin has said is made of conveniently twisted half-truths. Benjamin continues by saying that they cannot release those who are suspected of infection from isolation until a complete analysis of the sample, and that without the integration of the three powers, they are unable to permit their transfer to a hospital while he remains unsure of their "situation". He lets them know that the King's letter of authorization is being written as they speak, and those settling the matter will contact them once the letter has been signed and sealed, following which the letter can be retrieved.

Botobai steps close and looks Benjamin in the eye. He respectfully but intensely asks Benjamin if he is alright, saying that he looks rather pale. Benjamin emotionlessly says that there is no problem at all. As the two giants stare at each other in front of him, Kaiser wonders if Benjamin is pushing himself so hard because he had himself been infected, and in that case, he had 24 hours left at most, considering Halkenburg's progression. Kaiser hopes that they can stave the special martial law declaration off for one more day, after which they could see the light, and keeps in mind that he still has his final stronghold. Forty minutes have passed since special martial law was declared.
